findings.evidence: `rg -n "Every exchangeable sequence is contractable|hX_meas" code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Contractability.lean` shows the docstring at lines 89-91 and the required hypothesis at line 93.
findings.fix: Say that every exchangeable sequence with a.e. measurable coordinates is contractable, or otherwise mention the explicit measurability hypothesis.
findings.issue: `contractable_of_exchangeable` is documented as “Every exchangeable sequence is contractable,” but the theorem also requires `hX_meas : ∀ i, AEMeasurable (X i) μ`.

findings.evidence: Lines 124-127 document the dot-notation theorem as unconditional but the signature includes `(hX_meas : ∀ i, AEMeasurable (X i) μ)`.
findings.fix: Mirror the corrected wording from `contractable_of_exchangeable`, including the a.e. measurability condition.
findings.issue: `Exchangeable.contractable` repeats the same overclaim in its docstring while its statement still requires `hX_meas`.

summary: The added documentation mostly describes the new API, but the main theorem docstrings overclaim by dropping the separate measurability hypothesis required by the declarations.
